categoryDesc.financialFraud: Scams targeting money, investments, and financial transactions
categoryDesc.phishingSocial: Deceptive tactics to manipulate victims into revealing sensitive information
categoryDesc.technicalAttacks: Exploits and malware targeting systems, networks, and devices
categoryDesc.cryptoFraud: Scams and attacks targeting cryptocurrency and blockchain assets
categoryDesc.identityCrimes: Crimes involving identity theft, impersonation, and personal data misuse

fake-airdrop.name: Fake Airdrop Scam
fake-airdrop.description: Scammers promise free cryptocurrency tokens (airdrops) to lure victims into connecting their wallets or revealing private keys.
indicators: Requests to connect wallet to unknown websites
preventionTips: Never connect wallet to unverified airdrop sites
Verify airdrop announcements on official project channels
Use a separate wallet for testing airdrops
Be skeptical of free token offers

fake-crypto-exchange.name: Fake Crypto Exchange
fake-crypto-exchange.description: Fraudulent cryptocurrency exchanges that appear legitimate but steal deposits or prevent withdrawals.
preventionTips: Check for proper licensing and regulation

seed-phrase-theft.name: Seed Phrase Theft
seed-phrase-theft.description: Scammers trick victims into revealing their wallet seed phrase, giving them full access to all crypto assets.
indicators: Anyone asking for your seed phrase
Fake support requests for wallet issues
Social media offers to "help" with crypto
preventionTips: NEVER share your seed phrase with anyone
Legitimate support will never ask for seed phrases
Store seed phrases offline and securely
Be wary of unsolicited help offers
examples: Support agent asking for seed phrase to "fix" wallet
Twitter DM offering to recover lost crypto

sim-swap-attack.name: SIM Swap Attack
sim-swap-attack.description: Attackers transfer a victim's phone number to their own SIM to intercept 2FA codes and access crypto accounts.
indicators: Unable to make calls or send texts
preventionTips: Enable SIM lock with your carrier
Use authenticator apps instead of SMS 2FA
Contact carrier immediately if service is lost

crypto-giveaway-fraud.name: Crypto Giveaway Fraud
crypto-giveaway-fraud.description: Fake giveaways promising to multiply cryptocurrency if victims send a small amount first.
indicators: Promises to send back 2x or more of what you send
Requests to send crypto to "verify" address
preventionTips: Never send crypto to receive more crypto
Verify giveaway claims on official channels
Legitimate giveaways never require sending funds

defi-liquidity-scam.name: DeFi Liquidity Scam
defi-liquidity-scam.description: Fraudulent DeFi platforms that promise high yields for providing liquidity but steal the deposited funds.
indicators: Anonymous team with no track record
preventionTips: Verify smart contracts have been audited
Research the team and project thoroughly
Start with small amounts to test
examples: New yield farm with no audit

nft-minting-scam.name: NFT Minting Scam
nft-minting-scam.description: Fake NFT projects that collect minting fees but never deliver the promised NFTs or deliver worthless ones.
preventionTips: Verify the official website and social media
Check if artwork is original or stolen
Research the team and their previous work
Be cautious of minting during hype cycles
examples: Celebrity-endorsed NFT with no official confirmation

flash-loan-attack.name: Flash Loan Attack
flash-loan-attack.description: Exploits using flash loans to manipulate DeFi protocol prices and drain funds from liquidity pools.
preventionTips: Use protocols with proper oracle implementations
Stay informed about common exploit patterns

fake-staking-platform.name: Fake Staking Platform
fake-staking-platform.description: Fraudulent platforms that promise staking rewards but steal the deposited cryptocurrency.
indicators: No transparency about how rewards are generated
preventionTips: Verify the staking mechanism is legitimate
examples: New staking service with no track record

telegram-signal-group.name: Telegram Signal Group Scam
telegram-signal-group.description: Fake trading signal groups that provide false information to manipulate markets or steal funds.
preventionTips: Never share wallet keys with signal groups
Understand that no signals are 100% accurate

crypto-recovery-scam.name: Crypto Recovery Scam
crypto-recovery-scam.description: Services claiming to recover lost or stolen cryptocurrency that charge upfront fees without delivering results.
preventionTips: Legitimate recovery services don't charge upfront
examples: "We can recover your lost crypto for a fee"
Recovery service asking for wallet access

celebrity-impersonation.name: Celebrity Impersonation Scam
celebrity-impersonation.description: Fake accounts impersonating celebrities to promote crypto scams or giveaways.
indicators: Requests to send crypto to "verified" addresses
preventionTips: Verify account authenticity on social media
Check official celebrity channels for announcements
Be skeptical of celebrity crypto endorsements
Never send crypto based on celebrity recommendations
examples: Fake Elon Musk account promoting giveaway

malware-crypto-clipper.name: Malware Crypto Clipper
malware-crypto-clipper.description: Malware that replaces cryptocurrency wallet addresses in clipboard with attacker's addresses during transactions.
preventionTips: Always verify wallet addresses before sending

cross-chain-bridge-scam.name: Cross-Chain Bridge Scam
cross-chain-bridge-scam.description: Fake or compromised cross-chain bridges that steal cryptocurrency during transfers between blockchains.
examples: Fake bridge between Ethereum and BSC

fake-ico.name: Fake ICO Scam
fake-ico.description: Fraudulent Initial Coin Offerings that collect funds but never deliver the promised tokens or platform.
preventionTips: Research the team and their background
Look for a working product or MVP
Check for community reviews and feedback

dusting-attack.name: Dusting Attack
dusting-attack.description: Sending tiny amounts of cryptocurrency to many wallets to deanonymize owners and track their transactions.
indicators: Same small amount sent to many addresses
Attempts to link wallet to identity
preventionTips: Don't spend dust from unknown sources

crypto-mining-malware.name: Crypto Mining Malware
crypto-mining-malware.description: Malware that uses victim's computing resources to mine cryptocurrency without consent.

fake-wallet-update.name: Fake Wallet Update Scam
fake-wallet-update.description: Phishing attacks disguised as wallet software updates to steal credentials or install malware.
indicators: Requests to enter seed phrase during "update"
Poor grammar or spelling in update messages
preventionTips: Only download updates from official wallet websites
Verify update notifications through official channels
Never enter seed phrase during updates
Use wallet software's built-in update mechanism

ai-trading-bot.name: AI Trading Bot Scam
ai-trading-bot.description: Fake AI-powered trading bots that promise automated profits but steal funds or provide poor performance.
indicators: Requests for API keys with withdrawal permissions
preventionTips: Never grant withdrawal permissions to trading bots
examples: Trading bot requiring full API access

metaverse-investment.name: Metaverse Investment Scam
metaverse-investment.description: Fake metaverse projects or virtual real estate investments that collect funds without delivering value.
indicators: Promises of huge returns on virtual land
preventionTips: Verify the metaverse platform exists and works
Be skeptical of virtual land investment promises
Start with small investments to test
examples: Metaverse project with only a whitepaper

nft-rug-pull.name: NFT Rug Pull Scam
nft-rug-pull.description: NFT projects where developers abandon the project after collecting funds, leaving holders with worthless tokens.
preventionTips: Look for locked liquidity and roadmap
Be cautious of projects with anonymous teams
Join community to monitor project activity

fake-web3-job.name: Fake Web3 Job Scam
fake-web3-job.description: Fake job postings in Web3/crypto that trick applicants into paying fees or revealing sensitive information.
indicators: Requests for wallet addresses or private keys
preventionTips: Verify company legitimacy through official channels
Research the company and hiring manager

smart-contract-exploit.name: Smart Contract Exploit
smart-contract-exploit.description: Attackers exploiting vulnerabilities in smart contracts to steal funds or manipulate protocol behavior.

crypto-tax-scam.name: Crypto Tax Scam
crypto-tax-scam.description: Fraudulent crypto tax services that steal funds or provide incorrect tax advice leading to legal issues.
indicators: Requests for wallet access to "calculate taxes"
preventionTips: Never grant wallet access for tax purposes
Be skeptical of tax avoidance guarantees

qr-code-crypto.name: QR Code Crypto Scam
qr-code-crypto.description: Malicious QR codes that direct users to fake cryptocurrency websites or initiate unauthorized transactions.
preventionTips: Verify QR code source before scanning
Check URL after scanning before connecting wallet
Use QR codes only from trusted sources
examples: Physical QR code sticker promising free crypto

clipboard-hijacking.name: Clipboard Hijacking Malware
clipboard-hijacking.description: Malware that monitors and replaces cryptocurrency addresses in clipboard to redirect funds to attackers.
examples: Funds sent to attacker instead of intended recipient

crypto-romance.name: Crypto Romance Scam
crypto-romance.description: Romance scams where attackers build fake relationships to convince victims to invest in fake crypto platforms.
indicators: Online relationships moving quickly to investment topics
Pressure to invest on specific platforms
Refusal to video call or meet in person
Promises of shared wealth through crypto
preventionTips: Be skeptical of online relationships involving money
Never invest based on online relationship advice
examples: Romance scam with fake trading platform

fake-blockchain-project.name: Fake Blockchain Project Scam
fake-blockchain-project.description: Fraudulent blockchain projects that collect investments but never deliver promised technology or products.
preventionTips: Look for open-source code on GitHub

insider-trading.name: Insider Trading Manipulation
insider-trading.description: Market manipulation using insider information to profit at the expense of other traders.
preventionTips: Be cautious trading before major announcements

stablecoin-collapse.name: Stablecoin Collapse Scam
stablecoin-collapse.description: Fraudulent or mismanaged stablecoins that lose their peg, causing investors to lose funds.

phishing.name: Phishing
phishing.description: Fraudulent attempts to obtain sensitive information by disguising as trustworthy entities via electronic communication.
indicators: Generic greetings instead of personal names
preventionTips: Never share sensitive info via email

spear-phishing.name: Spear Phishing
spear-phishing.description: Targeted phishing attacks personalized for specific individuals or organizations using gathered information.
indicators: Knowledge of recent activities or relationships
preventionTips: Be skeptical of unexpected personalized messages

whaling.name: Whaling Attack
whaling.description: Spear phishing targeting high-profile individuals like executives to steal sensitive data or authorize transfers.
indicators: Requests for large transfers or sensitive data
Timing during busy periods or travel
preventionTips: Verify executive requests through multiple channels
examples: Fake CEO email requesting wire transfer

smishing.name: Smishing (SMS Phishing)
smishing.description: Phishing attacks via SMS messages attempting to trick recipients into revealing personal information.
preventionTips: Don't click links in unsolicited SMS
examples: Fake bank SMS with phishing link

vishing.name: Vishing (Voice Phishing)
vishing.description: Phone scams where attackers use voice calls to manipulate victims into revealing information or making payments.
indicators: Urgent threats about legal or financial issues
preventionTips: Hang up and call official numbers
Never share personal info over unsolicited calls

email-spoofing.name: Email Spoofing
email-spoofing.description: Forging email sender addresses to make messages appear from trusted sources.
indicators: Mismatched display names and email addresses

website-spoofing.name: Website Spoofing
website-spoofing.description: Creating fake websites that mimic legitimate sites to steal credentials or personal information.
preventionTips: Verify URLs carefully before entering data
Look for HTTPS and valid certificates

bec.name: Business Email Compromise (BEC)
bec.description: Sophisticated scams targeting businesses by compromising legitimate email accounts to conduct fraudulent transfers.
preventionTips: Verify payment changes through multiple channels
examples: Fake vendor requesting payment to new account

social-engineering.name: Social Engineering Attack
social-engineering.description: Psychological manipulation to trick people into giving up confidential information or access.
preventionTips: Follow security procedures regardless of pressure

tech-support-scam.name: Tech Support Scam
tech-support-scam.description: Fraudsters claiming to be tech support to gain access to devices or steal information.
preventionTips: Hang up on unsolicited tech support calls
Never grant remote access to unknown callers

fake-customer-care.name: Fake Customer Care Scam
fake-customer-care.description: Impersonating customer service representatives to steal information or money from victims.
indicators: Requests for account information or passwords
preventionTips: Contact customer service through official channels

courier-parcel.name: Courier/Parcel Scam
courier-parcel.description: Fake delivery notifications designed to steal personal information or payments.
indicators: Requests for payment to release packages
preventionTips: Track packages through official carrier websites

electricity-bill.name: Electricity Bill Scam
electricity-bill.description: Fake electricity bill notifications demanding immediate payment to avoid service disconnection.
preventionTips: Verify bills through official utility portals
Never pay via links in messages

aadhaar-scam.name: Aadhaar Scam
aadhaar-scam.description: Scams targeting Aadhaar card holders to steal personal information or commit identity fraud.
indicators: Requests for Aadhaar details via phone/email
preventionTips: Never share Aadhaar details via phone/email
Government never asks for sensitive info via phone

kyc-fraud.name: KYC Fraud
kyc-fraud.description: Fake KYC verification processes designed to steal personal documents and information.
indicators: Requests for document uploads via unofficial channels
preventionTips: Only complete KYC through official channels
Never share documents via unofficial methods

fake-government-scheme.name: Fake Government Scheme Scam
fake-government-scheme.description: Fraudulent schemes claiming to be government programs to collect personal information or payments.
indicators: Promises of government benefits or subsidies
Requests for personal information or payments
preventionTips: Verify schemes through official government websites
Government never asks for payments for benefits
Be skeptical of unsolicited scheme offers

insurance-fraud.name: Insurance Fraud
insurance-fraud.description: Scams targeting insurance policyholders to steal premiums or personal information.
indicators: Requests for policy details via unofficial channels
preventionTips: Verify insurance companies through official channels
Contact insurers directly for policy matters

lottery-scam.name: Lottery Scam
lottery-scam.description: Fake lottery winnings requiring victims to pay fees or provide personal information to claim prizes.
indicators: Notifications of winning lotteries never entered
Requests for payment to claim prizes
preventionTips: You can't win lotteries you didn't enter
Never pay to claim lottery prizes

charity-donation.name: Charity Donation Scam
charity-donation.description: Fake charity solicitations that collect money or personal information under pretense of helping causes.

fake-giveaway.name: Fake Giveaway Scam
fake-giveaway.description: Fake giveaways promising prizes to collect personal information or payments.
indicators: Prizes for actions that seem too easy
Requests for payment or shipping fees
preventionTips: Be skeptical of easy prize offers
Never share personal info for giveaways

fake-antivirus.name: Fake Antivirus Scam
fake-antivirus.description: Fake antivirus warnings designed to trick users into paying for unnecessary software or malware.

fake-job.name: Fake Job Scam
fake-job.description: Fake job postings designed to steal personal information, money, or commit identity theft.
indicators: Requests for personal information early in process
preventionTips: Be skeptical of offers without proper interviews
Verify job postings through official company channels
examples: Work from home scam requiring payment
Fake job offer requesting bank details

work-from-home.name: Work From Home Scam
work-from-home.description: Fake work-from-home opportunities that collect fees or personal information without providing real work.
indicators: Upfront fees for training or materials
preventionTips: Never pay upfront for work opportunities
Be skeptical of unrealistic earnings claims
Verify job legitimacy through multiple sources

deepfake-scam.name: Deepfake Scam
deepfake-scam.description: Using AI-generated fake videos or audio to impersonate people and manipulate victims.
indicators: Poor quality video or audio anomalies
Requests for money or sensitive information
Inability to verify identity through other means
preventionTips: Be skeptical of unusual requests from contacts
Look for visual/audio anomalies in videos
Establish verification protocols with important contacts

ai-voice-cloning.name: AI Voice Cloning Scam
ai-voice-cloning.description: Using AI to clone voices and impersonate family members or authorities to manipulate victims.
indicators: Unusual requests for money or information
Inability to verify through video call
preventionTips: Ask questions only real family would know
Be skeptical of urgent money requests

identity-theft.name: Identity Theft
identity-theft.description: Stealing personal information to commit fraud or other crimes in someone else's name.
indicators: Receiving bills for services not used
examples: Someone opening credit cards in your name

data-breach.name: Data Breach
data-breach.description: Unauthorized access to confidential information, potentially exposing personal data to criminals.
indicators: Password reset emails you didn't request
New accounts opened in your name
preventionTips: Use unique passwords for each account

data-theft.name: Data Theft
data-theft.description: Stealing digital information, including personal, financial, or corporate data.

credential-theft.name: Credential Theft
credential-theft.description: Stealing login credentials to access accounts and systems unauthorized.

sim-swap-fraud.name: SIM Swap Fraud
sim-swap-fraud.description: Fraudulently transferring a victim's phone number to attacker's SIM to intercept calls and messages.
indicators: Unable to make or receive calls
preventionTips: Use authenticator apps instead of SMS 2FA
Contact carrier immediately if service lost
examples: Carrier notification about unauthorized SIM change

otp-fraud.name: OTP Fraud
otp-fraud.description: Tricking victims into sharing One-Time Passwords to gain unauthorized access to accounts.
indicators: Requests to share OTP via phone/SMS
Multiple OTP requests you didn't initiate
preventionTips: Legitimate services never ask for OTP sharing

insider-threat.name: Insider Threat Attack
insider-threat.description: Security risks from employees, contractors, or business partners with inside access.

qr-scam.name: QR Code Scam
qr-scam.description: Malicious QR codes that direct victims to fake payment sites or initiate unauthorized transactions.
indicators: QR codes promising discounts or free items
preventionTips: Only scan QR codes from trusted sources
Verify QR code destination before scanning
examples: QR code sticker over legitimate code

upi-fraud.name: UPI Fraud
upi-fraud.description: Fraudulent transactions using UPI (Unified Payments Interface) to steal money from victims.
indicators: Requests to scan unknown QR codes
preventionTips: Don't allow screen sharing during transactions
examples: UPI PIN theft via social engineering

internet-banking-fraud.name: Internet Banking Fraud
internet-banking-fraud.description: Unauthorized access to bank accounts through online banking channels to steal funds.
preventionTips: Verify bank communications through official channels

atm-skimming.name: ATM Skimming
atm-skimming.description: Installing devices on ATMs to capture card data and PINs to clone cards and steal funds.

card-cloning.name: Card Cloning
card-cloning.description: Creating duplicate cards using stolen card data to make unauthorized transactions.
indicators: Card used in locations you haven't visited
preventionTips: Use chip cards instead of magnetic stripe
Use virtual cards for online purchases
examples: Cloned card used at different location
Skimmer data used to create duplicate card

online-shopping-fraud.name: Online Shopping Fraud
online-shopping-fraud.description: Fake online stores or sellers that collect payments without delivering goods.
indicators: Prices too good to be true
preventionTips: Use secure payment methods with buyer protection

auction-fraud.name: Auction Fraud
auction-fraud.description: Fake auction listings or bidding schemes that collect payments without delivering items.
indicators: Sellers refusing to use platform protection
Fake bidding to drive up prices
Item descriptions that don't match photos
preventionTips: Be skeptical of deals that seem too good

fake-loan-app.name: Fake Loan App Scam
fake-loan-app.description: Fraudulent lending apps that charge excessive fees, harass borrowers, or steal personal information.
preventionTips: Never pay upfront fees for loans

investment-scam.name: Investment Scam
investment-scam.description: Fraudulent investment schemes promising high returns with little or no risk.

ponzi-scheme.name: Ponzi Scheme
ponzi-scheme.description: Fraudulent investment operation that pays returns to earlier investors using funds from newer investors.
indicators: Consistent high returns regardless of market
preventionTips: Be skeptical of consistent high returns

pyramid-scheme.name: Pyramid Scheme
pyramid-scheme.description: Fraudulent business model that recruits members with promise of payments for enrolling others.
indicators: Focus on recruitment over product sales
preventionTips: Focus on product value, not recruitment

rug-pull.name: Rug Pull Scam
rug-pull.description: Developers abandon projects after collecting funds, leaving investors with worthless tokens.

honeypot-scam.name: Honeypot Scam
honeypot-scam.description: Smart contracts or trading pairs that appear legitimate but prevent selling or withdrawing funds.
indicators: High trading volume but no sellers

pump-and-dump.name: Pump and Dump Scam
pump-and-dump.description: Inflating asset prices through false statements, then selling at the peak to cause losses for other investors.
indicators: No fundamental reason for price increase
preventionTips: Don't invest based on hype alone
Take profits when targets are met

wallet-drainer.name: Wallet Drainer Scam

topicLayout.reported: 🚨 Been targeted? Report immediately — every minute counts.

hero.title: Understand UPI scams, protect your PIN, and pay safely.
hero.desc: This module helps you spot fake collect requests, screen-share traps, and phishing payment prompts — without using any real payment APIs.

why.title: Why this matters
why.desc: UPI fraud is one of the fastest-growing payment scams in India. This lab helps you identify the red flags before you approve a transaction.
why.bullet1: Fake collect requests often appear like refunds, prizes, or customer support.
why.bullet2: If an app asks for your UPI PIN to receive money, treat it as a high-risk scam.
why.bullet3: Always verify the recipient, the amount, and the expected flow before approving payments.

sections.title: Explore the UPI Fraud learning path
sections.desc: Go deeper into scam awareness, real case learning, interactive UPI checks, and reporting best practices.

awareness.title: UPI Fraud Awareness
awareness.desc: Learn common UPI scams, collect request tricks, and safe transaction habits.

caseStudy.title: Case Study
caseStudy.desc: Follow a real-world UPI scam timeline and learn how the victim could have stayed safe.

demo.title: Interactive Demo
demo.desc: Practice spotting scammy UPI requests, suspicious payees, and unsafe payment prompts.

report.title: Report UPI Fraud
report.desc: Get step-by-step guidance for preserving evidence and reporting payment fraud fast.

facts.title: Fast facts
facts.bullet1: Never share your UPI PIN or OTP with anyone.
facts.bullet2: Validate the payee VPA and amount before approving collect requests.
facts.bullet3: Use official bank apps or UPI apps, not links sent by strangers.

watch.title: What to watch
watch.bullet1: Unexpected refund or prize requests that ask for payment approval.
watch.bullet2: Screen sharing or remote-control calls that ask you to open UPI apps.
watch.bullet3: Impersonation of banks, merchants, or government officials.

reportQuick.title: Report quickly
reportQuick.bullet2: File at cybercrime.gov.in with UPI transaction details.
reportQuick.bullet3: Keep screenshots, UPI IDs, and bank reference numbers.

awarenessPage.title: UPI Fraud Awareness
awarenessPage.desc: Protect your UPI account by learning key scam patterns, approval traps, and safe payment rules.

qr.title: QR Scam Simulator
qr.desc: Train against realistic QR payment fraud, fake UPI redirects, tampered stickers, and phishing pages. Inspect indicators, earn XP, and build a habit of verifying before paying.
qr.trainingDesc: Each route keeps the same neon SOC interface and reuses the QR score engine.

route1.title: Realistic QR scam simulator
route1.desc: Scan metro refunds, parking fines, charity posters, and cafe menus. Open indicators to learn what makes a QR dangerous.

route2.desc: Make rapid calls on UPI prompts and QR destinations. Final results show accuracy and readiness.

route3.title: Awareness and Indian cyber law
route3.desc: Learn how to report QR fraud, preserve evidence, and recognize IT Act sections commonly applied to online cheating.

route4.title: QR fraud case study
route4.desc: Walk through a realistic incident timeline and practice spotting the decision points that matter.

route5.title: Incident report guide
route5.desc: Collect transaction IDs, screenshots, URLs, and timestamps before escalation.

qr.footer: CyberShield QR module | Verify destination, payee, amount, and intent before approving.

awarenessPage.title: QR Scam Safety & Laws
awarenessPage.desc: Learn how scammers weaponize QR codes to bypass url protections and how Indian cyber law penalizes QR cheating.
awarenessPage.card1Desc: If an app asks for UPI PIN, assume money can leave your account. Refunds and prize credits do not need your PIN.
awarenessPage.card2Desc: Fake pages can copy brand colors. Check HTTPS, domain spelling, official suffixes, and whether the page asks for OTP or card data.
awarenessPage.card3Desc: Parking meters, fuel pumps, cafes, and counters can be relabeled. Ask staff or use the official app when the sticker looks altered.
awarenessPage.law1: IT Act Section 66C relates to identity theft and credential misuse.
awarenessPage.law2: IT Act Section 66D relates to cheating by personation using computer resources.
awarenessPage.law3: IPC/BNS cheating provisions may also be relevant depending on the complaint.
awarenessPage.tip1: Call 1930 quickly for financial cyber fraud assistance.
awarenessPage.tip2: File a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in with screenshots and UTR numbers.
awarenessPage.tip3: Share the VPA, phone number, URL, timestamp, bank, and transaction ID.
awarenessPage.tip4: Ask your bank or UPI app to block/freeze the transaction trail immediately.

awarenessPage.title: AI Deepfake Awareness
awarenessPage.desc: Understand AI-generated fake media, deepfake blackmail, identity spoofing, and verification methods.
awarenessPage.timelineDesc: A practical response flow for suspicious video calls, voice notes, livestreams, and celebrity or executive impersonation.
awarenessPage.step1Desc: Do not click, pay, share OTPs, or send files while pressure is high.
awarenessPage.step2Desc: Use a known phone number, official website, or internal directory to confirm identity.
awarenessPage.step3Desc: Look for lip-sync drift, repeating gestures, lighting mismatch, unnatural voice tone, and odd urgency.
awarenessPage.step4Desc: Report to platform support, cyber cell, bank, or internal security team with preserved evidence.
awarenessPage.checklist1: Use multi-person approval for payments and account changes.
awarenessPage.checklist2: Agree on code words for urgent family or executive requests.
awarenessPage.checklist3: Keep official reporting links bookmarked before an incident.
awarenessPage.checklist4: Train teams to question media that demands secrecy or speed.
awarenessPage.footer: Awareness playbooks reduce risk before detection tools are needed.
